entries.title: Missing Rate Limiting
entries.summary: Sensitive operations with no rate limits

indicators: No per-block or per-epoch limits on value-moving operations
No cooldown periods between critical actions
Unbounded operation frequency for minting/withdrawing/parameter changes
design_context.description: Sensitive operations such as minting, withdrawing, and parameter changes have no rate limits. An attacker or compromised admin can drain or disrupt in a single transaction or rapid sequence.

entries.title: Ordering Assumptions
entries.summary: Design assumes specific operation order without enforcing it

indicators: Price updates assumed to arrive before dependent operations
No commit-reveal scheme where ordering matters
design_context.description: The design assumes operations execute in a specific order but doesn't enforce it. MEV searchers, frontrunners, or concurrent users can reorder transactions to extract value.

entries.title: Critical state reporting piggybacked on user activity creates unbounded staleness for inactive components
entries.summary: A design where a safety-critical counter reaches its consumer only by hitchhiking on user-initiated transactions will silently drift for components with low activity. The safety property depends on activity cadence that cannot be guaranteed.

indicators: A safety counter is reported to its consumer only via payload attached to user-initiated messages
The protocol serves components with variable activity (multi-chain deployments, multi-pool, multi-gauge)
The underlying counter accumulates continuously regardless of whether reports are dispatched
design_context.description: When a critical counter can only reach its consumer by piggybacking on user activity, the safety property degrades silently for components with infrequent activity. The counter keeps growing on the source; the consumer operates on stale data. Dormancy condition is not 'zero activity' -- it is any period where activity cadence falls below the rate of counter growth. On multi-component systems, at least one component is typically in this state. The gap becomes the blast radius for any exploit targeting the consumer-side cap.

entries.auditor_notes: Confirmed by team as accepted risk pending further design review. Mitigation options: keeper/periodic baseline dispatch, rate-of-activity monitoring.

confirmed_by.context: Confirmed in cross-chain emission-accounting design review
